response:
## PR Review: 01-messy-ts-app

Categorising 22 findings against an empty baseline. Cutoffs: **block** when severity ≥ 0.85 *or* a single finding combines a real correctness risk with a high agent_risk; **warn** for medium-severity issues an agent could plausibly compound on the next edit; **ignore** for low-severity style noise (still worth fixing eventually, not worth blocking the merge).

### Block merge (1)

| ID | Charge | Sev | agent_risk | Why block |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| crime_00001 | God Function (`generateInvoice`, 204 lines) | 0.9 | 0.63 | High severity *and* the function is in the billing path. 3.4× the domain threshold, mixed responsibilities → next agent edit will silently miss interactions. Extract before any further change lands. |

### Warn (13)

These are real concerns but landable if tracked. Most cluster around two themes: **agents will be misled by this code** and **multiple-source-of-truth drift**.

| ID | Charge | Sev / agent_risk | Why warn (not block) |
|---|---|---|---|
| crime_00008 | False Identity (`calculateRefundPreview` has 3 side-effect signals: `saveRefundAudit`, `sendRefundEmail`) | 0.55 / 0.52 | The closest call to "block" — a name lying about side effects is exactly the trap `crimes` is built to surface. Borderline; flag it loudly in the PR. |
| crime_00002 | Missing Agent Context (no AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/SKILL.md) | 0.6 / 0.61 | Cheap fix, big agent payoff. Worth doing now but won't break anything if it slips. |
| crime_00004 | Temporal Recklessness in `src/billing.ts` (7× clock reads) | 0.6 / 0.53 | Wall-clock in domain code = flaky tests + monkey-patching. Inject a clock. |
| crime_00005 | Temporal Recklessness in `src/date.ts` (5× clock reads) | 0.6 / 0.61 | Same problem; `test_gap=1` (no tests) raises agent_risk further. |
| crime_00007 | String Sprinkles ("enterprise" across 4 files / 4 areas) | 0.5 / 0.6 | One more copy lands and it's duplicated policy. Centralise before the next edit. |
| crime_00009 | Concept Alias Drift (tenant/account/org/team/workspace across 5 files) | 0.55 / 0.58 | An agent extending one vocabulary will duplicate logic that already exists under another name. |
| crime_00010 | God File (`billing.ts` 310 lines) | 0.6 / 0.5 | Same file as crime_00001 — fixing the God Function will likely shrink this naturally. |
| crime_00011 | Logic in Comments ("Only owners can refund enterprise plans unless billing support approves") | 0.5 / 0.5 | Hidden prose rule on a refund path. Encode it as a guard or test. |
| crime_00012 | Duplicated Navigation Source (`/settings/billing`: "Plans" vs "Billing") | 0.6 / 0.57 | Pair this with crime_00014 — same destination, multiple labels. Fix once. |
| crime_00013 | TODO density in `src/todo.ts` (14 markers, 424/1k LOC) | 0.4 / 0.57 | The file *is* TODOs. Either it's a deliberate parking lot (move to issue tracker) or it shouldn't ship. |
| crime_00014 | Route Metadata Drift (`/settings/billing` labelled 4 different ways) | 0.6 / 0.56 | Sibling to crime_00012 — align route + nav + page title in one pass. |
| crime_00015 | Docs-Code Drift (`docs/teams.md` → missing `./setup.md`) | 0.35 / 0.51 | Low severity but `agent_risk > 0.5` because `test_gap=1` — agents will follow the stale link. Trivial to fix. |
| crime_00006 | Test That Proves Nothing ("renders the enterprise plan", 0 asserts) | 0.45 / 0.46 | A green test with no assertion lies to humans *and* agents about what's covered. Borderline-block; warn because deleting it is acceptable. |

### Ignore (8)

Low severity, low agent_risk, or duplicates of warnings already flagged. Not worth blocking the PR — file as cleanup or leave for `crimes scan --all` later.

| ID | Charge | Sev / agent_risk | Why ignore |
|---|---|---|---|
| crime_00016 | Weak test ("creates an invoice", weak matcher) | 0.25 / 0.34 | Lowest agent_risk in the report. Worth strengthening but not a merge gate. |
| crime_00017 | Option Bag Junk Drawer (`describeCheckout` reads 6 props off generic `options`) | 0.3 / 0.48 | Style-shaped; agent_risk under 0.5. Name the shape next time you touch it. |
| crime_00018 | Return Shape Roulette (`resolvePaymentResult`, 3 shapes, 0% key overlap) | 0.3 / 0.48 | Same module as 00017, same vibe. Annotate the return type when convenient. |
| crime_00019 | Negative Flag Maze (`!disableBilling && !skipRetry`) | 0.25 / 0.47 | Local readability nit. |
| crime_00020 | TODO density in `billing.ts` (4 markers, 12.9/1k LOC) | 0.2 / 0.4 | Already covered by the God File / God Function warnings — fixing those clears most of these. |
| crime_00021 | Orphaned `/account/subscription` | 0.4 / 0.46 | The three orphan routes are below the warn bar individually. But: |
| crime_00022 | Orphaned `/team` | 0.4 / 0.46 | …they're the same three files that drove crime_00009 (alias drift). |
| crime_00023 | Orphaned `/workspace/members` | 0.4 / 0.46 | If the alias-drift cleanup picks a canonical term, two of these three routes should be deleted as part of it. Track there. |

### One-line PR verdict

**Request changes.** Block on crime_00001 (God Function in billing). The 13 warnings cluster into three cleanup passes — extract billing, pick canonical names (enterprise / tenant / `/settings/billing`), inject a clock — and the 8 ignores mostly fall out for free once those land.

per_question.question: Did the agent's block/warn/ignore split align with severity (high → block, medium → warn, low → ignore)?
per_question.reasoning: The agent's categorization aligns well with severity thresholds. The single block is crime_00001 with severity 0.9 (highest, ≥0.85 cutoff stated explicitly). Warnings cluster in the 0.4-0.6 severity range (medium), and ignores are mostly 0.2-0.4 (low). The split is principled with a stated cutoff rule. Minor friction: a couple of warn items (crime_00013 at sev 0.4, crime_00015 at sev 0.35) fall into low-severity territory but are escalated to warn — though the agent explicitly justifies these by agent_risk, which is the correct framework. The ignore bucket includes some sev 0.4 items (orphan routes) which technically sit at the warn boundary, but the agent justifies bundling them under crime_00009. Overall a clean, defensible mapping.

per_question.question: Did the agent justify deviations by citing scores.agent_risk specifically?
per_question.reasoning: The agent explicitly cites scores.agent_risk to justify deviations from a pure severity-based split. Examples: crime_00015 ('Low severity but agent_risk > 0.5 because test_gap=1'), crime_00013 (sev 0.4 promoted to warn with agent_risk 0.57 noted), crime_00005 ('test_gap=1 raises agent_risk further'), and the explicit cutoff rule names agent_risk as a tiebreaker ('block when severity ≥ 0.85 or a single finding combines a real correctness risk with a high agent_risk'). Every warn-tier finding lists its agent_risk score in the table, and the ignore tier notes 'agent_risk under 0.5' as justification. The agent_risk framework is consistently invoked, exactly as the scenario asks.
